de.uka.ilkd.key.rule

Class ContractRuleApp

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.AbstractBuiltInRuleApp
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.AbstractContractRuleApp
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.ContractRuleApp
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    IBuiltInRuleApp, RuleApp

    public class ContractRuleApp
extends AbstractContractRuleApp
    Represents an application of a contract rule. Currently, this is only used for applications read in from a proof file; fresh applications are represented as regular BuiltInRuleApps. (yes, I know that this is ugly - BW)

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from class de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.AbstractContractRuleApp

        instantiation

      • Fields inherited from class de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.AbstractBuiltInRuleApp

        builtInRule, ifInsts, pio

        Description copied from interface: IBuiltInRuleApp
        returns true if tryToInstantiate may be able to complete the app

        Returns:

      • tryToInstantiate

        public ContractRuleApp tryToInstantiate(Goal goal)
        Description copied from interface: IBuiltInRuleApp
        Tries to complete the rule application from the available information. Attention: Do neither add GUI code to the rules nor use this method directly Instead ask the implementation of the UserInterfaceControl to complete a built-in rule. Returns a complete app only if there is exactly one contract. If you want a complete app for combined contracts, use forceInstantiate instead. For an example implementation see e.g. UseOperationContractRule or UseDependencyContractRule.
        Specified by:
        tryToInstantiate in interface IBuiltInRuleApp
        Specified by:
        tryToInstantiate in class AbstractContractRuleApp
